description: Comprehensive reference of global, cultural, and religious holidays mapped to the Global Horizon Standard.

holidays.name: New Year (Equinox)
holidays.description: The first day of the year, aligned with the spring equinox. A celebration of renewal and the astronomical start of the cycle.

holidays.name: Earth Day
holidays.description: Global celebration focusing on environmental protection and planetary awareness.

holidays.name: Labor Day
holidays.description: International day honoring the labor movement and workers worldwide.

holidays.name: Summer Solstice Gala
holidays.description: Marks the longest day of the year (northern hemisphere). A festival of light and energy.

holidays.name: Autumn Equinox Festival
holidays.description: The balance point of the year. Day and night are equal. A celebration of harvest and equilibrium.

holidays.name: United Nations Day
holidays.description: Commemorates the creation of the United Nations and global diplomacy.

holidays.name: Winter Festival
holidays.description: A seasonal celebration near the end of November, replacing region-specific winter holidays with a shared global moment.

holidays.name: Winter Solstice Ceremony
holidays.description: The shortest day (northern hemisphere). Marking the return of the light.

holidays.name: International Women's Day
holidays.description: Global day celebrating the social, economic, cultural, and political achievements of women.

holidays.name: Luna Moon Month
holidays.description: The 13th and final month — Luna. A time of reflection, closure, and preparation before the Aurora Week and the new year.

holidays.name: Aurora Festival
holidays.description: The Aurora Week — seven days of global pause, reflection, maintenance, and celebration before the new year begins.

holidays.name: Christmas
holidays.description: Christian holiday commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ.

holidays.name: Halloween / Reformation Day
holidays.description: Eve of All Hallows / Commemoration of the Protestant Reformation.

holidays.name: Valentine's Day
holidays.description: Feast of Saint Valentine, globally recognized as a day of romance and love.

holidays.name: Day of the Dead (Dia de los Muertos)
holidays.description: Mexican holiday honoring deceased loved ones.

holidays.name: Easter Sunday
holidays.rule: First Sunday after the first full moon on or after GHS 01.01
holidays.description: Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Wanders between early March and late April.

holidays.name: Ramadan (Start)
holidays.description: A month of fasting, prayer, reflection, and community. Shifts backwards through the GHS calendar by roughly 11 days per year.

holidays.name: Eid al-Fitr
holidays.description: Festival of Breaking the Fast, marking the end of Ramadan.

holidays.name: Eid al-Adha
holidays.rule: 10th day of Dhu al-Hijjah (Islamic Lunar Calendar)
holidays.description: Feast of the Sacrifice, honoring the willingness of Ibrahim to sacrifice his son.

holidays.name: Passover (Pesach)
holidays.description: Commemorates the liberation of the Israelites from slavery in Egypt.

holidays.name: Rosh Hashanah
holidays.description: The Jewish New Year.

holidays.name: Yom Kippur
holidays.description: Day of Atonement, the holiest day of the year in Judaism.

holidays.name: Hanukkah
holidays.description: Festival of Lights, commemorating the rededication of the Second Temple.

holidays.name: Holi
holidays.description: Festival of Colors, celebrating spring, love, and new life.

holidays.name: Diwali
holidays.description: Festival of Lights, symbolizing the spiritual victory of light over darkness.

holidays.name: Vesak
holidays.rule: First full moon in May equivalent (Buddhist Lunar Calendar)
holidays.description: Commemorates the birth, enlightenment, and death of Gautama Buddha.

holidays.name: Lunar New Year
holidays.rule: 1st day of the 1st month (Chinese Lunisolar Calendar)
holidays.description: Celebrates the beginning of a new year on the traditional lunisolar calendar.
